get.description: Activity reason as its own read-model — the snapshot counterpart to the live status update (same computation, single owner).

Tasks of an asset as a filtered collection.

Without query parameters, all tasks are returned. aggregation/period restrict
to a time range (via calculated_end), sort=price_per_unit sorts by price per
unit (order asc/desc) and implies only FINISHED tasks (other states have no
price_per_unit), limit caps the list. The cheapest task of a period is returned
by ?aggregation=month&period=YYYY-MM&sort=price_per_unit&order=asc&limit=1.

post.description: Create a support ticket for an asset and automatically open a tracking issue.

Initiate a relinking flow for an existing user with a linked asset.
Relinking allows users to re-establish their connection with their provider
without going through the complete onboarding process. This is useful when users
change their password or need to refresh their authentication.

get.description:
Get the feature configuration for authenticated users.

This endpoint allows regular authenticated users to retrieve the current
feature flags, including the scheduling feature status. This is particularly
useful when the app starts and opens a stream connection, as it needs to
know the current state of the scheduling feature.

TaskStateEnum.title: TaskStateEnum
TaskStateEnum.description: State enum for API responses. CANCELLED is not exposed to the client.

ActivityReason.title: ActivityReason
ActivityReason.description: Reason why an asset is currently active.

ActivityStatus.title: ActivityStatus
ActivityStatus.description:
Read-model: why the asset is currently active — or not.

Intentionally separated from the Asset/AssetState: `activity_reason` is a value
derived across three bounded contexts (telemetry, optimization, policy) and does
not belong in the telemetry aggregate. Snapshot via
GET /assets/{id}/activity_status, live updates via the status stream.

Money.title: Money
Money.description:
A monetary amount.

``value`` is serialized as a decimal string (full precision, no exponent); the
currency unit is fixed. There is intentionally no sub-unit ``per`` — that is
carried by the field name (e.g. ``avg_price_per_unit``).

Quantity.title: Quantity
Quantity.description:
A quantity in generic units. Decimal-backed; ``value`` is serialized as a JSON
number (unlike ``Money``: no string precision required).

TaskSortFieldEnum.title: TaskSortFieldEnum
TaskSortFieldEnum.description: Whitelist of sortable fields for the task collection query.

PriceMetric.title: PriceMetric
PriceMetric.description:
A monetary amount with a proportional ratio (``pct``, within the period) and a
percentage trend (``trend``, compared to the previous period).
